title: universal pkg installer
body:
### Feature request description

some people are downloading the wrong .pkg installer due to podman.io redirecting to amd64 installer as it's hard to know which arch is operated from a browser.

Then, they're unable to start a podman machine as it's trying to run the wrong qemu binary as the arch is not the same

So if the recommended pkg from the website would be the universal .pkg it would solve many problems

### Suggest potential solution

there is a [podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.9.3/podman-installer-macos-amd64.pkg) and a [podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.9.3/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg)

probably there should be a -universal.pkg

That would also help Podman Desktop that will embed the universal pkg in its universal distribution

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: @ashley-cui @baude PTAL. It looks like this is possible but we'd need to do the merging on an actual Mac.
I'd agree this is a good idea and looks possible but prob needs some time to read the mac packaging docs.
Fwiw, universal binaries can be generated cross-platform using https://github.com/randall77/makefat (we are using it in `crc`).
